dependency_notes: Strictly sequential per architecture.md Story Order (supersedes spec.md §13 ordering ambiguity — none exists here, both agree S0..S5). S0 must land before S1 touches app/src/lib/theme.ts (baseline is captured against the unmodified file). S1 is pure scaffold with no registry import — no behavior change, so it has no direct runtime dependency on S0 but must not regress the file S0 just read. S2 depends on S1's schema/buildChakraTheme/fontUnion/contrast/treatments modules existing, and on S0's committed baseline for its AC-PARITY-1/2 assertions. S3 depends on S2's registry stub (same shape, replaces it) and on S1's schema/contrast/fontUnion for the validation suite. S4 depends on S2's registry + S1's treatments library. S5 depends on the field/behavior surface being final (S1-S4 complete) to document it accurately. No two stories touch package.json's prebuild wiring simultaneously (only S3), and stories 3 and 4 share no simultaneous write target per architecture.md.
mock_resolution_plan: MOCK-02-001 (hand-authored registry.generated.ts stub introduced in S2) is resolved in S3 when generate-theme-registry.mjs regenerates the same file programmatically and --check confirms idempotence (byte-equivalent shape). No mock survives past S3; mocks-registry.json should be updated by the lead when S3 lands.

seams.data: Per-theme manifest object (identity, color scales/semantic tokens, typography, shape, treatments, behavior flags)

fields.name: order
fields.type: number (unique positive integer across all themes)

fields.name: colorScheme
fields.type: 'light'|'dark' (descriptive only, never trusted by the contrast gate)

fields.name: colors
fields.type: 5x Scale10 (brand/success/warning/danger/neutral) + semantic tokens (appBg, backgroundImage?, surfaceBg, surfaceMutedBg, surfaceRaisedBg, borderSubtle, borderStrong, textMuted, textStrong, status bg/border/text, buttonColorScheme)

fields.name: treatments
fields.type: { card: ElevationName; button: ElevationName; nav: ElevationName; surface: SurfacePatternName; iconSet: IconSetName; banner: string; contentPanel?: ContentPanelName; overrides?: RawOverrides }

invariants: id === folder basename for every theme
order values are unique positive integers across all manifests
manifest.ts contains no functions and no @chakra-ui/react runtime import (pure, serializable data)
computed WCAG contrast ratio >= 4.5:1 for every required (text, surface) pair in §6.7, from actual hex values only

seams.data: Chakra theme override object and the cached, referentially-stable getChakraTheme(id) result

destination.artifact: app/tests/unit/theme.test.ts (or themes-validation.test.ts) AC-PARITY-1/AC-PARITY-2 assertions against theme-baseline.generated.ts

invariants: pick(getChakraTheme(id), ALLOWLIST) deep-equals the committed pre-refactor baseline for each of calm/playful/lego/minecraft/flower
getChakraTheme(id).components.Button.variants is a non-empty object (full-extend guard: catches a regression that returns the bare override instead of extendTheme(...))
getChakraTheme(id) is a stable reference across calls (module-level cache, no re-render churn)

seams.data: The generated theme registry: which themes exist, their sort order, and the deduplicated font union

source.artifact: app/src/themes/registry.generated.ts (committed hand-authored stub: APP_THEMES, AppThemeName, THEME_FONTS)

intermediaries.artifact: app/scripts/generate-theme-registry.mjs (regenerates the same file from folder names; --check verifies idempotence)

destination.artifact: app/src/themes/index.ts, app/src/lib/theme.ts, app/src/types/index.ts, app/pages/_document.tsx, app/pages/profile.tsx (readers of APP_THEMES / AppThemeName / THEME_FONTS)

fields.name: AppThemeName
fields.type: string union built from theme folder names

fields.name: APP_THEMES
fields.type: Record<AppThemeName, ThemeManifest>, keys equal the folder set, sorted by ascending order

invariants: Object.keys(APP_THEMES) exactly equals the set of app/src/themes/*/ folders containing manifest.ts (no drift)
APP_THEMES key order reflects ascending order (calm=1, playful=2, lego=3, minecraft=4, flower=5)
THEME_FONTS matches buildThemeFonts(all manifests) recomputed at test time (no metadata drift)
generate-theme-registry.mjs --check exits zero against the committed file (idempotent) and never imports/evaluates TypeScript

seams.data: Font declarations and the byte-exact Google Fonts CSS2 URL

invariants: families are sorted alphabetically in the URL
axis encoding: ital-only -> Family:ital@0;1; weights-only -> Family:wght@w1;w2; both -> Family:ital,wght@0,w1;1,w1; neither -> Family; suffix &display=swap

seams.data: Resolved per-surface treatment styles consumed by Layout.tsx and index.tsx

destination.artifact: app/src/hooks/useThemeStyles.ts (return value consumed by Layout.tsx and pages/index.tsx)

invariants: exactly these six fields are returned; no visualStyle, no isFunTheme
bannerDecorStyle keeps boxProps and style split (Chakra drops data-URI backgroundImage when merged into BoxProps)
per-theme values match the pre-refactor useThemeStyles.ts output exactly

stories.name: baseline-capture
stories.title: Capture the pre-refactor Chakra theme baseline
stories.description: Add app/scripts/capture-theme-baseline.mjs, which imports the currently-exported getChakraTheme (with zero edits to app/src/lib/theme.ts) and picks the ALLOWLIST subtrees (colors, semanticTokens, fonts, fontSizes, radii, styles, config, components.<Name>.defaultProps) for calm, playful, lego, minecraft, and flower. Commit the resulting snapshot as app/tests/unit/theme-baseline.generated.ts. This is the parity fixture every later refactor step is asserted against, so it must be captured before story S1 touches theme.ts.

includes: Capture ALLOWLIST subtrees for all five current themes from the unmodified getChakraTheme
excludes: Any edit to app/src/lib/theme.ts or any other src/ file
Any change to rendered theme output
Writing the AC-PARITY-1 assertion test itself (that lands in S2, consuming this baseline)

stories.notes: Hard prerequisite for AC-PARITY-1 (S2) per spec §6.8/architecture.md Implementation Constraint #4. lighter_path is false: all file targets are executable .mjs/.ts codegen, not markdown-class files.

stories.name: themes-scaffold
stories.title: Scaffold themes/ pure-data and transform modules
stories.description: Create app/src/themes/schema.ts (zod ThemeManifestSchema + inferred ThemeManifest type + validateManifest()), app/src/themes/buildChakraTheme.ts (pure buildThemeOverride(manifest) + cached getChakraTheme = extendTheme(override), lifting createScale() from theme.ts:71), app/src/themes/fontUnion.ts (buildThemeFonts/buildFontLinkHref), app/src/themes/contrast.ts (wcagRatio/evaluateThemeContrast), and app/src/themes/treatments/{elevation,iconSets,patterns}.ts. app/src/themes/index.ts is created as a pass-through that re-exports from app/src/lib/theme.ts unchanged — no registry import yet, no behavior change to any of the six existing consumer files.

includes: app/src/themes/index.ts as a no-op pass-through to lib/theme.ts
excludes: Any edit to app/src/lib/theme.ts's exported behavior or any of its 6 consumer files
The generator script and validation test (S3)

stories.notes: owning_module is set to buildChakraTheme as the anchor module for this scaffold story; schema, fontUnion, contrast, and treatments/* are created alongside it in the same story because spec.md §13 and architecture.md's Story Order both bundle them into one 'Scaffold' story. See story-planner retrospective: this is a genuine tension with the one-story-one-module design principle, not a re-derivation of architecture.md's module boundaries. AC-STRUCT-5 (public API surface stability across S1-S4) is assigned here because S1 is the first story touching the themes/ public surface and the schema requires every story to carry at least one AC; spec.md/architecture.md's story split assigns zero ACs to this story natively.

stories.name: migrate-five-themes
stories.title: Migrate the five existing themes into manifests + registry stub
stories.description: Author app/src/themes/{calm,playful,lego,minecraft,flower}/manifest.ts, translating the explicit 10-step color scales, exact semantic-token hex values, typography, and banner SVGs from today's theme.ts and useThemeStyles.ts field-for-field (no re-tuning). Commit a hand-authored app/src/themes/registry.generated.ts stub exposing APP_THEMES/AppThemeName/THEME_FONTS with the same shape the S3 generator will later emit. Wire app/src/themes/index.ts to the registry, app/src/lib/theme.ts to re-export it, and app/src/types/index.ts to import AppThemeName from the registry. Assert byte-identical parity against the S0 baseline and the Minecraft/font-size invariants.

includes: app/src/lib/theme.ts becomes a thin re-export of themes/index.ts
app/src/types/index.ts imports AppThemeName from the registry
Parity assertions: pick(getChakraTheme(id), ALLOWLIST) toEqual baseline; full-extend guard; minecraft contentSurface/fontSizes invariants
excludes: The codegen generator script and --check mode (S3)
The full themes-validation.test.ts suite (contrast/drift/metadata/order-uniqueness/font-URL/zod-boundary land in S3)

introduces_mocks.description: Hand-authored registry.generated.ts stub stands in for the codegen-emitted registry until the generator lands in S3. It is a real, working implementation (not a stub that fakes behavior) but is manually maintained rather than derived from folder names, so it is tracked until the generator supersedes it.

stories.notes: AC9/AC12 per spec §13 and architecture.md Story Order (unambiguous — both documents agree these land in S2).

stories.name: generator-and-validation-gate
stories.title: Add the build-time generator, prebuild gate, and validation test suite
stories.description: Add app/scripts/generate-theme-registry.mjs (globs theme folders containing manifest.ts, emits registry.generated.ts as a folder-name-derived scaffold only, performs structural checks only, never imports TypeScript, supports --check idempotence mode). Wire app/package.json's prebuild script to run the generator then `vitest run tests/unit/themes-validation.test.ts` before next build. Add app/tests/unit/themes-validation.test.ts covering: zod schema parse + id===folder + id regex, the real-hex contrast gate (§6.7 pairs, AA 4.5:1), drift (folder set vs APP_THEMES keys), metadata-freshness (order-sorted keys + THEME_FONTS recomputation), order uniqueness, font-URL byte parity, and the zod-boundary architectural scan.

includes: app/package.json: add the prebuild script (generate-theme-registry.mjs && vitest themes-validation). NOTE: zod itself was added as a direct devDependency in S1 (where schema.ts + its test first import it) per Codex S1 finding — S3 only owns the prebuild wiring here.
app/tests/unit/themes-validation.test.ts: schema parse, id/folder regex, contrast gate, drift, metadata-freshness, order-uniqueness, font-URL parity, zod-boundary scan
Regenerating registry.generated.ts via the generator, replacing the S2 hand-authored stub (MOCK-02-001 resolution)

stories.notes: AC8b -> this story per lead resolution (architecture.md's Story Order table is silent on AC8b, not in conflict with spec.md §13, which assigns it here). AC17 -> this story per the same resolution (architecture.md's table omits it; spec.md §13 assigns it to this story alongside AC14, and this is the story that authors the zod-boundary architectural test, so the assignment is also independently correct on the merits). | CODEX-S0 (carried, resolve in S3): the spec §6.4 "metadata-freshness" test as literally worded (recompute-from-manifests vs committed-registry-that-also-recomputes) is vacuous — both sides derive from the same live manifests. The real drift it targets is already covered by AC-FONT-1 (fontLoad edits fail the committed font-URL parity) and AC8b (duplicate order). S3 must EITHER materialize a committed order/fonts snapshot via a TS-capable codegen (mirrors the S0 baseline pattern) so the freshness test gains teeth, OR narrow AC8 given the sibling coverage. Decide with the concrete registry shape in front of you; escalate only if a genuine product tradeoff surfaces.

stories.name: wire-consumers
stories.title: Wire useThemeStyles, ThemeIcon, profile picker, _document fonts, and i18n cleanup
stories.description: Update app/src/hooks/useThemeStyles.ts to resolve manifest.treatments.* through the treatments library and return exactly the six consumed fields (drop visualStyle/isFunTheme and the ThemeVisualStyle re-export). Update app/src/components/ThemeIcon.tsx to resolve icon ids via manifest.treatments.iconSet against treatments/iconSets.ts. Update app/pages/profile.tsx to render manifest label/description (en, de-fallback) via listThemes() with hidden-theme filtering. Update app/pages/_document.tsx to render its font <link> href from buildFontLinkHref(THEME_FONTS). Remove the 10 calm...flowerDescription keys from app/src/lib/i18n.ts while keeping themeHeading/themeDescription/active/currentTheme.

includes: normalizeThemeName fallback + hidden-id self-normalization + legacy mood field round-trip
excludes: app/src/components/Layout.tsx and app/pages/index.tsx must remain unchanged (only consume the same return shape)

stories.notes: AC15 -> this story per lead resolution (architecture.md's Story Order table is silent on AC15 for this story; spec.md §13 explicitly lists AC15 alongside AC10/AC11/AC13 here, and storage.ts/useMoodTheme.tsx — the two of AC-STRUCT-5's six named consumers not touched by any story — are only provably unbroken once this story's consumer changes land, making S4 the natural place to also close out AC-STRUCT-5's cross-cutting claim; it is tracked instead under S1 per the schema's one-story-per-AC constraint, see S1 notes).

stories.name: authoring-guide
stories.title: Publish the theme authoring guide and README
stories.description: Write docs/themes/authoring-guide.md documenting: folder structure; the complete ThemeManifest field reference (required/optional, types, id regex, order uniqueness); the exact contrast pairs and AA 4.5:1 threshold; typography and FontLoad; the treatment catalog and the overrides escape hatch with its stated limits; banner/asset options; localization; status behavior; how to run validation locally; one fully worked sample theme; and the gotchas list (static export; dark themes must pass contrast or set contentSurface; backgroundImage must stay decorative/low-opacity). Write a short app/src/themes/README.md that links to it.

includes: docs/themes/authoring-guide.md (full field reference, contrast pairs, FontLoad, treatment catalog + overrides limits, banner/asset options, localization, status, validation instructions, worked sample, gotchas)
app/src/themes/README.md (short pointer to the authoring guide)
excludes: Any code change to app/src/themes/* or app/src/lib/*
Any change to the manifest schema, generator, or validation test

stories.notes: Despite being markdown-only (files_code is empty) and AC-DOC-1 being fully specified (no placeholder/ellipsis/trailing-question, uses MUST throughout), lighter_path computes to false: neither docs/themes/ nor app/src/themes/README.md exists on disk at planning time, so the new_file signal (AC-STORY-3, 'files list references a path that does not exist on disk') fires and forces the conservative default. This contradicts the spawn brief's suggestion that S5 was a 'strong lighter_path candidate' -- flagged in the retrospective below since the deterministic rule and the advisory guidance disagree for pure-greenfield doc stories. | CARRIED from S3 (VQ-S3-023): the authoring guide (AC-DOC-1 "how to run validation") MUST include a repeatable AC-DEP-2 build-abort verification recipe — e.g. "edit a manifest id to an invalid value (Uppercase), run `npm run prebuild`, confirm it exits non-zero and `next build` does not run, then restore." The property is verified; this documents the repeatable check.
